channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Small town still healing after losing 20 young men on D-Day
item.description: On July 17, 1944, the small town of Bedford, Virginia with a population of just 3,200 would learn that 20 men died in the D-Day invasion.
item.dc:creator: Marielena Balouris, NewsNation

item.title: Texas man overcomes testicular cancer twice, pursues dream of fatherhood
item.description: "Testicular cancer can grow very quickly. People notice this difference over about one to two weeks," Dr. Kyle Keyes said.
item.dc:creator: Jose Torres, NewsNation

item.title: Biggest source of new Floridians and Texans last year was other countries
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — The biggest source of new residents to Florida and Texas, the two U.S. states with the largest number of new residents last year, was other countries.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A father and son are both indicted on murder charges in a mass school shooting in Georgia
item.description: A grand jury in Georgia has indicted a father and son in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Winder.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, ‘South Pacific’ star, dies at 93
item.description: Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of fire pits recalled over possible 'flame jetting'
item.description: Thousands of fire pits are being recalled due to "flame jetting and fire spreading" hazards that have resulted in over a dozen burn injuries, according to the U.S. Consumer Protection Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from the last full fundraising quarter before Election Day
item.description: Federal candidates have submitted their reports for what was the final full fundraising quarter before Election Day, offering a snapshot of their financial status with just three weeks to go.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Bret Baier spar over immigration at outset of Fox News interview
item.description: Vice President Harris on Wednesday sparred at length with Fox News anchor Bret Baier over immigration in a rare sit-down with the network. Baier opened the interview by asking Harris how many immigrants had crossed the southern border during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Alabama to execute man for killing 5 in what he says was a meth-fueled rampage
item.description: Alabama prepared Thursday to put to death a man who admitted to killing five people with an ax and gun during a drug-fueled rampage in 2016 and dropped his appeals to allow his execution to go forward. Derrick Dearman, 36, is scheduled to be executed by lethal injection at 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: BY KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's foreign minister says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, Fox 5

item.title: Person whose DNA was found on collar of dead puppy tied to fire hydrant has died
item.description: After a pit bull puppy was found dead, tied to a fire hydrant in Richmond's East End in November of 2023, investigators have released new information and closed the case.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) privately slammed former President Donald Trump as “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and “a despicable human being” after the 2020 election, according to a biography of the senator set to be released later this month.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: 3 dead, several injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and several others were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, Fox 5

item.title: The Biden administration has now canceled loans for more than 1 million public workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A student loan cancellation program for public workers has granted relief to more than 1 million Americans — up from just 7,000 who were approved before it was updated by the Biden administration two years ago.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Embattled prosecutors Gascon, Foxx endorse Harris, say Trump 'would make us all less safe'
item.description: Divisive attorneys George Gascon of Los Angeles and Kim Foxx of Chicago endorsed Vice President Kamala Harris on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal money to help states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton nears $2 billion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government has approved nearly $2 billion in assistance across six states to help people hit hard by hurricanes Helene and Milton, the White House said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dog spotted on top of Great Pyramid of Giza
item.description: Talk about a long walk. A paraglider getting an aerial view of the Great Pyramids of Giza in Egypt on Monday was surprised to see a dog atop the iconic giant pyramid.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: After 36 years, accused killer in Oregon cold case to stand trial
item.description: An autopsy revealed Deborah Atrops was physically assaulted and strangled to death. She was 30. Then, the case went cold.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KRON4

item.title: California health care workers get a pay bump under a new minimum wage law
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Some of the lowest-paid health care workers in California will get a pay bump Wednesday under a state law gradually increasing their wages to at least $25 an hour.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, ABC 4

item.title: Do you keep money in payment apps? Here's why you probably shouldn't
item.description: As the use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Pixel Watch 3: Bigger screen, smarter features
item.description: Google Pixel Watch 3 is going big this year! This time around, there's a new option for a larger screen size—45 millimeters. It feels much roomier than the old screen size, which is still available.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Michelle Obama will headline an Atlanta rally aimed at boosting voter turnout
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former first lady Michelle Obama will headline a rally in Atlanta a week before the Nov. 5 election alongside celebrities and civic leaders focusing on engaging younger and first-time voters, as well as voters of color. The Oct.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where is the 'best restroom' in the U.S.? In a Utah gas station
item.description: Maverik often advertises itself as "Adventure's First Stop" -- but now it can say it's home to "America's Best Restroom," too.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Can Apple Watch 10 be used as a medical device?
item.description: It's increasingly clear that the future of the Apple Watch isn’t just as an alternative to cell phones.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Survivors and relatives in Maine's deadliest shooting start the process of suing the Army
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Lawyers for 100 survivors and relatives of victims of the deadliest shooting in Maine history have begun the formal process of suing the U.S. Army for what they say was a failure to act to stop the reservist responsible for the crime, attorneys announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Some Social Security recipients will receive an extra check next month
item.description: It may be hard to believe, but November is fast approaching. With it will come Election Day, Thanksgiving, and, for some seven million Supplemental Security Income beneficiaries, an extra check. It's all thanks to a quirk in the 2024 calendar.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: NHC monitoring 2 disturbances in the Atlantic and Caribbean | Tracking the Tropics
item.description: With Florida's Gulf Coast left reeling after back-to-back major hurricanes, our Tracking the Tropics team explains what could spare the region from experiencing a third storm within a month.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, Fox 5

item.title: In Missouri, Halloween night signs were required at the homes of sex offenders. Until now
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Many states have laws designed to keep children away from convicted sexual offenders on Halloween night, such as curfews for those on offender registries and requirements to keep their porch lights off.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspected 'murder hornet' found in Washington State
item.description: The northern giant hornet, sometimes nicknamed the "murder hornet," has been known to attack and kill entire honeybee hives.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Convicted killer Susan Smith charged after speaking with filmmaker
item.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WSPA) - Convicted killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a disciplinary charge weeks before her parole hearing. Smith, of Union County, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of her children in Oct. 1994. The SC Department of Corrections confirmed with 7NEWS that on Aug.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: NJ Transit operator killed, passengers injured after train hits tree
item.description: An NJ Transit train operator is dead and nearly two dozen passengers are injured after a train struck a tree on the tracks Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, Fox 5
